diagrams.title: Fixed Partition Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows memory divided into fixed partitions and how processes are loaded
elements: Partition 3 (150KB) - P2 loaded with internal fragmentation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding fixed partition allocation and internal fragmentation

diagrams.title: External Fragmentation Example
diagrams.description: Illustrates scattered free memory blocks that cannot satisfy a large request
elements: Process D (200KB) - cannot fit despite 90KB total free
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how external fragmentation prevents memory allocation despite sufficient total free space

diagrams.title: Bounds Register Address Translation
diagrams.description: Shows how base and limit registers work for memory protection and address translation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory protection mechanism in contiguous allocation

diagrams.title: Memory Compaction Process
diagrams.description: Demonstrates before and after memory compaction
elements: Before: Process A | Hole 20KB | Process B | Hole 10KB | Process C | Hole 30KB
After: Process A | Process B | Process C | Hole 60KB (combined)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how compaction combines scattered free memory into one block
